free_text: Reaction mechanisms of fast neutrons on stable Mo
7-MeV Van De Graaff Accelerator in Geel.
Two different experimental facilities were used to
perform the irradiations. The irradiations in the
energy range from 16.1 to 20.5 MeV were done at the
IRMM 7 MeV Van de Graaff accelerator in Geel (data
presented in this Entry), while the measurements
between 13.4 and 14.9 MeV were performed at the KRI
Neutron Generator NG-400 in St. Petersburg (data
Programs KINEMA and ENERGYSET were used
Samples were irradiated in 0 degrees direction
Monitor foils and samples were placed directly
on detector cap. Photo-peak efficiency was determined
using calibrated standard sources supplied by
Al, Nb, Fe, and In foils of 13 mm diameter were
attached on both sides of a sample stack and served
acquisition mode - for neutron flux monitoring.
Natural samples were prepared by punching discs of
13 mm diameter from metallic foil or by cutting
squares of 1 cm2 size from sheets.

combines effects of deuteron straggling in Ti/T
layer and range of angles subtended by sample.
